text: Adam (Kingma & Ba 2015, ICLR) combines momentum and adaptive learning rates per-parameter, becoming the most widely used optimizer in deep learning. It computes running averages of gradients (m_t) and squared gradients (v_t).

AdamW (Loshchilov & Hutter 2019, ICLR) fixes Adam's weight decay implementation by decoupling it from the adaptive gradient update, improving generalization and becoming the default optimizer for Transformer training.

Lion (Chen et al. 2023, Google) is a simpler optimizer using only sign operations, discovered through program search. It achieves 2-5× memory savings over AdamW while matching or exceeding its performance.
